hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME
hard.next_step: 75% Confidence 1st Jul Recieved 60 questions in a "clarifications" request- due by15th July 6th Jun- RFI submitted on time- strong proposal and now we wait for shortlist. Reaching out to STE to check if reference from Shuck is possible? We need STE to support from back end as a high profile Temasek subsid- Pinged Wee Keong and Anthony May 25th- clarified this deal is for the HOLDING group only (not the full Temasek group of companies) May 25th RFI due 28th - Tanvi driving - require QIT and reference request for Workday, more clarifications May 11th RFI invitation arrive today- the cycle will begin in earnest- ck to reach out to STE for guidance and support to navigate execo May 05- Confirmation RFI will coming in following 2 weeks. Apr 27: Following a 1 year of engagement, RFI finally expected by end of mid of May. Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities. Apr 20: Request for NDA signature to receive RFI. Mar 20: Finalizing the RFI focused on S2C. Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger). Process: RFI/RFP Sep, Oct/ final decision Dec. STE informed about process and happy to support. Shirley & Wing On invited at AI RT. Feb 06: Meeting with Adam to review our AI capabilities. He mentioned RFI is coming in months. 13rd Oct: No additional engagement expected at this stage. BD to KIV 18th Aug: Meeting last week with P2P Manager. Demo is expected by Sep. No formal initiative at this stage but S2C emerging interest. 15th Jul: Engaging with Infosys through their JV with Temasek (Infosys Compass) to penetrate the account. Mid Term engagement 17th Mar: Relaunch by mail (+ invite to Horizon) last week, no response so far 03rd Mar: BD to KIV 08th Nov: BD to KIV. No identified initiative at this moment. 25th Oct: no response. ask BD to KIV. 21st Oct: no further interest shown with Zycus at this stage. No information on possible tender. Send Plan24 Invitation. 11th Oct: No answers from my last mails 27th Sep: BD to reengage with Forrester Wave and get update next week. 19th Sep: update from On Wing next week (confirmation if they open a selection process) 04th Sep: . Temasek downloading collateral from Showpad . Scheduling checkpoint with prospect by end of Sep 27th Aug: no opportunity confirmed by Temasek at this stage . Temasek asks for some time to review internally the scope of their initiative (with S2C or CLM for instance). . Creating a share space (video on auctions shared on Aug 27). . BD sharing whitepapers about features/ Gen AI/ Microsoft integration to main procurement stakeholders (Bi-weekly). . Checkpoint with customer by Sep 20. 16th Aug- Demo part 2 scheduled by Aug 22. Special interest with S2C but no clear initiative identified at this stage. 26th Jul- S2P Overview demo part 1 with all executive procurement team.
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 6 Feb 2025 rep note: 'Meeting with Adam to review our AI capabilities. He mentioned RFI is coming in months.' Per the 27 Apr 2026 note: 'Following a 1 year of engagement, RFI finally expected by end of mid of May. Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities.' Buyer-side Adam has explicitly asked to review Zycus's AI capabilities ‚Äî this is a buyer-originated AI focus.
• Per the 11 May 2026 note: 'RFI invitation arrive today- the cycle will begin in earnest- ck to reach out to STE for guidance and support to navigate execo.' Per 20 Mar 2026: 'Finalizing the RFI focused on S2C. Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger. Process: RFI/RFP Sep, Oct/ final decision Dec. STE informed about process and happy to support. Shirley & Wing On invited at AI RT.' Buyer-side Shirley and Wing On invited to an AI Round Table event ‚Äî additional buyer-side AI engagement signal.
• Confidence is High because the buyer has specifically asked to review AI capabilities (Feb 2025), AI-themed RT attendance is invited, and the RFI lands May 2026 with Zycus positioning around 'AI embedded I2O capabilities'. Caveats: Only ML iAnalyze in product scope (no Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA yet), competitive set is heavy (GEP/Coupa/iValua/Workday/Jaggaer), and the RFI itself is just landing ‚Äî no formal AI scoring criteria visible. Score 7 reflects buyer-stated AI focus with the formal cycle just beginning.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live formal RFI: Temasek returned 60 clarification questions on 1 Jul (due 15 Jul), so the deal is engaged, but the 18 Dec close is not credible with shortlist, RFP, selection, negotiation and contracting all still ahead in a five-vendor race.
north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 2024-07-25, 711 days old. Moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation 2026-05-11 when the RFI invitation landed (56 days in current stage). RFI submitted 2026-06-06; buyer returned 60 clarification questions 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15, so engagement is live within the last 5 days, not silent. Close 2026-12-18 is 165 days out. Buyer-stated process (Next Step 2026-03-20): RFI/RFP Sep-Oct, final decision Dec. To close by 2026-12-18 the whole chain (shortlist, RFP, vendor selection, negotiation, InfoSec, contracting) must complete after a December decision, which is not feasible. The date will slip into Q1 2027 even if Zycus wins.
evidence: Buyer engagement is LIVE: Temasek returned a 60-question RFI clarification request on 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15 (Task 2026-07-01, Next Step 2026-07-01), and confirmed on 2026-06-15 they were reviewing Zycus's RFI responses.
RFI submitted on time 2026-06-06 after the invitation arrived 2026-05-11 (Next Step); NDA signed between Temasek and Zycus 2026-04-20 (Task 2026-05-11).
Buyer-stated timeline (Next Step 2026-03-20): 'Process: RFI/RFP Sep, Oct/ final decision Dec.' A December decision leaves no room to contract by 2026-12-18.

Forecast Best Case is indefensible: five-vendor RFI (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer, Workday per Next Step 2026-03-20), no economic buyer mapped above Decision Maker Wing Yu Onz, no active champion, no quantified ROI.

Scope clarified 2026-05-25 to the Temasek HOLDING group only, not the ~300-company portfolio (Task 2026-05-25), and the amount was cut 600k to 310k on the same day, which bounds deal size and C-level visibility.
Owner is VP APAC Carl Kimball, so the account is already senior-owned; no manager escalation is warranted.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case (cost savings, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, payback) has been co-created with Temasek across the engagement. The Next Step history from 2024 through 2026-07-01 records process and AI-capability discussion but no buyer-stated financial impact, and the Feb 2026 Adam Kang meeting was AI-capability focused, not value quantification. The 2026-06-06 RFI response presumably carried a value proposition but no activity records a buyer acknowledgement of quantified metrics. In a $325k five-vendor RFI, the rival who lands a CFO-grade savings and cost-of-inaction story wins budget buy-in, so quantifying value with procurement before the shortlist is the highest-leverage move.

sources: OpportunityContactRole Wing Yu Onz Decision Maker
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner above procurement is identified. Contact Roles name Wing Yu Onz (Director, Procurement) as Decision Maker and PRIMARY, plus VP-level evaluators Shirley Tan, Hui Yeeng Choong and Jiahui Gan, but none is confirmed to control the commercial decision or budget. The Next Step references reaching out to STE (a Temasek-related sponsor) 'to support from back end as a high profile Temasek subsid' (Next Step 2026-05-11) and to STE for a reference from Shuck (Next Step 2026-07-01), which shows awareness of the need for senior backing but no mapped C-level sponsor. Identifying and reaching the person who owns the S2C budget before the shortlist decision is a ranked move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is a formal S2C RFI scored against a structured questionnaire: the buyer explicitly asked for a QIT reference and Workday integration proof (Task 2026-05-25) and referenced Temasek's IT Security Standards & Policies for the non-functional questions (Task 2026-05-12), so integration fit and InfoSec compliance are confirmed criteria. AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration is a buyer-originated criterion, evidenced by Adam Kang asking to review Zycus AI capabilities (Next Step 2026-02-06) and the AI Round Table invitations (Next Step 2026-03-20). The full weighting of the 60 clarification questions issued 2026-07-01 is not yet visible, so the relative importance of price versus AI versus integration is inferred rather than confirmed.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, procurement-led competitive process: NDA signed 2026-04-20, RFI invitation 2026-05-11, RFI submitted 2026-06-06, submission under review from 2026-06-15, and a 60-question clarification round issued 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15. The buyer's own roadmap (Next Step 2026-03-20) is RFI/RFP in Sep-Oct with a final decision in December. Wing Yu Onz (Decision Maker) and Adam Kang (Assistant VP, coordinating the RFx and access) run the procurement side; scope was formally bounded to the Temasek holding group on 2026-05-25. The near-term gate is surviving the clarification round into the shortlist.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but real: an NDA was executed between Temasek and Zycus on 2026-04-20 (Task 2026-05-11), and the RFI package invokes Temasek's IT Security Standards & Policies for compliance (Task 2026-05-12), signalling an InfoSec review will gate any award. No MSA/SOW, legal redline, or procurement-channel detail is yet visible because the deal sits pre-shortlist. Visibility will come once the buyer names finalists and opens security due diligence; asking procurement for the post-shortlist paper and InfoSec timeline would create it.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer is modernising Source-to-Contract for the Temasek holding group, with emerging interest that hardened into a formal RFI after roughly a year of engagement (Next Step 2026-04-27: 'Following a 1 year of engagement, RFI finally expected'). Buyer-originated AI focus is a confirmed driver: Adam Kang asked to review Zycus's AI capabilities (Next Step 2026-02-06) and Shirley and Wing On were invited to an AI Round Table (Next Step 2026-03-20). The specific operational or financial pain (current tooling gaps, manual effort, compliance risk) has not been articulated in quantified terms, so pain is inferred from the RFI scope rather than confirmed by the buyer.

sources: OpportunityContactRole Adam Kang / Wing Yu Onz / Shirley Tan
champion.narrative: No active champion with internal-selling behaviour or economic-buyer access is documented. Adam Kang (Assistant VP, Procurement) is the most engaged buyer contact, coordinating RFI access and sharing process intelligence, but that is coaching, not advocacy. Wing Yu Onz (Decision Maker) and Shirley Tan (VP Procurement) accepted AI Round Table invitations (Next Step 2026-03-20) yet no record shows them pushing back on rivals or introducing a budget sponsor. Converting Adam Kang or Wing Yu Onz into a champion who drives the shortlist internally is a prerequisite before the December decision.

competition.narrative: A five-vendor formal RFI: GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer and Workday (Next Step 2026-03-20: 'Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger'). Workday is both a bidder and the buyer's likely ERP, so a stay-with-Workday path is a live threat, underscored by the buyer's request for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference (Task 2026-05-25). GEP is the closest size and scope peer. No buyer statement yet ranks or favours any vendor, so the field is neutral and open; Zycus's wedge is AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration versus bolt-on AI, which must be made concrete in the clarification responses due 2026-07-15.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved from a dormant year-long nurture into a live formal RFI in Q2 2026: NDA (20 Apr), RFI invitation (11 May), on-time RFI submission (6 Jun), buyer review confirmed (15 Jun), and a 60-question clarification round (1 Jul, due 15 Jul). Scope and amount were reset on 25 May when the buyer confirmed the RFI covers the Temasek holding group only, not the ~300-company portfolio.

Forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case; stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation; RFI invitation received
NDA executed between Temasek and Zycus

competitive_position.summary: A five-vendor formal RFI (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Jaggaer, Workday) with no buyer-stated lean yet, so the field is open. GEP is the strongest current threat as the closest size and scope peer in the same RFI; Workday is the incumbent-ERP threat because the buyer asked for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference on 2026-05-25, raising a stay-with-Workday risk. Coupa, iValua and Jaggaer are active RFI participants without differentiated buyer signal. Zycus's wedge is AI-embedded intake-to-orchestration versus bolt-on AI.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a live five-vendor RFI heading to a December decision, refreshed for the 15 Jul clarification deadline.
flags: Make the 60 clarification answers the AI differentiator: lead with embedded Merlin/I2O versus GEP and Coupa bolt-on AI, since AI is the buyer-originated criterion (Next Step 2026-02-06).
Neutralise the Workday stay-put path now: land a concrete Workday-integration proof point and the QIT reference the buyer asked for on 2026-05-25 before shortlist.
Map and reach a budget owner above Wing Yu Onz: the deal is single-threaded into procurement with no economic buyer, risky in a $325k C-suite-visible RFI.

champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Adam Kang coordinates the RFI and shares process intelligence (coaching), and Wing Yu Onz is the passive Decision Maker; neither is documented introducing a budget sponsor, pushing back on rivals, or driving Zycus internally. In a five-vendor RFI heading to a December decision, developing a champion who owns the shortlist narrative is the priority.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Adam Kang, Wing Yu Onz, Shirley Tan); Next Step 2026-02-06 and 2026-03-20

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the buyer-originated wedge (Adam Kang asked to review AI capabilities, AI Round Table invites), and Zycus is positioning on AI-embedded I2O, but only ML iAnalyze is in product scope with no Merlin/Agentic AI attached, so the agentic story is under-told relative to the RFI opportunity.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate reflects a genuine buyer-stated AI focus (Adam Kang's capability review request, AI Round Table attendance) against a heavy competitive set and no formal AI scoring criteria yet visible; no Avoma calls exist to test whether the field is under or over-rated.

items.detail: Buyer's own roadmap (RFI/RFP Sep-Oct, decision Dec) leaves no room to contract by the 18 Dec close; the date will slip to Q1 2027.
No economic buyer or budget owner above procurement is mapped; the deal is single-threaded into Wing Yu Onz and Adam Kang.
Buyer asked for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference, raising a stay-with-Workday-ERP risk if unanswered.
Scope narrowed to the holding group only and amount cut from 600k to 325k on 2026-05-25, a defensive contraction that bounds deal size and executive visibility.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and rep-and-buyer two-way through the RFI channel (clarifications 1 Jul), but there is no active champion, no economic buyer, and no quantified value case, so confidence in a win is moderate and confidence in the December date is low.
items: Live: 60-question RFI clarification round issued 2026-07-01, due 2026-07-15
On-time RFI submission 2026-06-06 with buyer confirming review 2026-06-15
Warn: five-vendor field with no buyer-stated lean and no mapped budget owner

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Temasek is a live, on-time five-vendor RFI where Zycus is well-positioned on AI but has no champion, no mapped economic buyer, and no quantified value case; the 60-question clarification answers due 15 Jul are the next gate, and the only realistic path is surviving into the shortlist while the 18 Dec close slips to Q1 2027.

win_position.text: Win sits moderate for an early Formal Evaluation stage: Zycus submitted a strong on-time RFI and owns the buyer-originated AI wedge, but it holds down because the field is still five vendors wide with no buyer-stated lean (Next Step 2026-03-20).

Zycus is credibly in the race: AI is a buyer-originated criterion (Adam Kang asked to review AI capabilities, Next Step 2026-02-06) and the RFI was submitted on time with the buyer confirming review on 2026-06-15.
No economic buyer above Director Wing Yu Onz is mapped and there is no active champion pushing Zycus internally, so we cannot yet influence the shortlist decision (Next Step 2026-05-11).
The buyer asked for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference (Task 2026-05-25), signalling a live stay-with-Workday-ERP alternative that could displace a best-of-breed award.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is real and buyer-driven: Temasek issued a 60-question clarification round on 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15 after confirming RFI review on 2026-06-15, a genuine pull, not rep chasing.
The buyer's own timeline (RFI/RFP Sep-Oct, decision Dec per Next Step 2026-03-20) means the next real milestone after clarifications is months out, so momentum is procedural rather than accelerating.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer committed process capital: NDA signed 2026-04-20, SharePoint/portal access granted, and a formal clarification cycle opened, all consistent with a serious evaluation.
Commitment is procurement-led and procedural: no senior sponsor has stepped forward, scope was cut to the holding group only on 2026-05-25, and no Zycus-specific preference has been voiced.

deal_risk.text: The 18 Dec close is indefensible against the buyer's own December-decision roadmap; the date will slip to Q1 2027 even in a win (Next Step 2026-03-20).
Single-thread and power risk: no economic buyer mapped, no champion, and no quantified ROI in a $325k five-vendor RFI raise the odds of losing the shortlist to a better-sponsored rival.

champion.evidence: No active champion; Adam Kang coaches on process only, Wing Yu Onz is a passive Decision Maker (Next Step 2026-03-20).

economic_buyer.evidence: No budget owner above procurement mapped; only STE back-end support referenced (Next Step 2026-05-11).

business_case.evidence: No quantified savings/ROI co-created with the buyer across the engagement (Next Step history).

timeline.evidence: Buyer December decision cannot contract by the 18 Dec close (Next Step 2026-03-20).

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer statement favours Zycus; the buyer is running a neutral five-vendor RFI and asked for a Workday integration reference (Task 2026-05-25), with no Zycus-specific preference voiced.

business_case.evidence: No quantified savings, cycle-time, or ROI has been co-created with Temasek; the engagement records AI-capability and process discussion only (Next Step history 2024-2026).

scope_change.detail: On 2026-05-25 the buyer clarified the RFI covers Temasek itself, not the ~300 portfolio companies (Task 2026-05-25), and the amount was cut 600k to 310k the same day (Field history 2026-05-25), later nudged to 325k on 2026-05-26. A defensive contraction that bounds deal size and reduces C-level visibility.

items.expected_effect: Converts latent senior backing into active sponsorship ahead of the RFP round.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 2026-05-25 the buyer clarified the RFI covers Temasek itself, not the ~300 portfolio companies (Task 2026-05-25), and the amount was cut 600k to 310k the sam (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 16 is carried by momentum 59 vs stage-expected 52; commercial strength +0.40 (weight 5). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 2026-05-25 the buyer clarified the RFI covers Temasek itself, not the ~300 portfolio companies (Task….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 8 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 9 is anchored on the stage win (16), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Temasek is a live, on-time five-vendor RFI where Zycus is well-positioned on AI but has no champion, no mapped economic buyer, and no quantified value case; the 60-question clarification answers due 15 Jul are the next gate, and the only realistic path is surviving into the shortlist while the 18 Dec close slips to Q1 2027.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Win sits moderate for an early Formal Evaluation stage: Zycus submitted a strong on-time RFI and owns the buyer-originated AI wedge, but it holds down because the field is still five vendors wide with no buyer-stated lean (Next Step 2026-03-20).

Zycus is credibly in the race: AI is a buyer-originated criterion (Adam Kang asked to review AI capabilities, Next Step 2026-02-06) and the RFI was submitted on time with the buyer confirming review on 2026-06-15.
No economic buyer above Director Wing Yu Onz is mapped and there is no active champion pushing Zycus internally, so we cannot yet influence the shortlist decision (Next Step 2026-05-11).
The buyer asked for Workday integration proof and a QIT reference (Task 2026-05-25), signalling a live stay-with-Workday-ERP alternative that could displace a best-of-breed award.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 16.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum is real and buyer-driven: Temasek issued a 60-question clarification round on 2026-07-01 due 2026-07-15 after confirming RFI review on 2026-06-15, a genuine pull, not rep chasing.
The buyer's own timeline (RFI/RFP Sep-Oct, decision Dec per Next Step 2026-03-20) means the next real milestone after clarifications is months out, so momentum is procedural rather than accelerating.

bullets.text: The 18 Dec close is indefensible against the buyer's own December-decision roadmap; the date will slip to Q1 2027 even in a win (Next Step 2026-03-20).
Single-thread and power risk: no economic buyer mapped, no champion, and no quantified ROI in a $325k five-vendor RFI raise the odds of losing the shortlist to a better-sponsored rival.

Commitment is procurement-led and procedural: no senior sponsor has stepped forward, scope was cut to the holding group only on 2026-05-25, and no Zycus-specific preference has been voiced.
